title: Belfer Center's Korea Project Co-Leads Planning for South Korean President's Historic Harvard Visit and Speech
summary: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol visited Harvard on April 28 and delivered a major policy speech at Harvard Kennedy School’s JFK Jr. Forum. Following his speech, President Yoon joined Harvard Distinguished Service Professor Joseph Nye for a conversation about security and soft power derived from the country's cultural strength. He also took questions from the large audience of students and others attending the event in person and online. The Belfer Center’s Korea Project worked closely with Harvard University colleagues and Korean Consulate General counterparts to arrange for the visit and the Harvard speech, the first for a sitting South Korean president. The Kennedy School's Institute of Politics and the Korea Project co-sponsored the historic speech.
body: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol visited Harvard on April 28 and delivered a major policy speech at Harvard Kennedy School’s JFK Jr. Forum. Following his speech, President Yoon joined Harvard Distinguished Service Professor Joseph Nye for a conversation about security and soft power derived from the country's cultural strength. Yoon also took questions from the large audience of students and others attending the event in person and online. The Belfer Center’s Korea Project worked closely with Harvard University colleagues and Korean Consulate General counterparts to arrange for the visit and the Harvard speech, the first for a sitting South Korean president. The Kennedy School's Institute of Politics and the Korea Project co-sponsored the historic speech. Prior to President Yoon’s visit to Harvard, where he was welcomed by a Harvard delegation including President Lawrence Bacow, Vice Provost Mark Elliott, Korea Institute Director Nicholas Harkness, and Korea Project Director John Park , Yoon was in Washington, D.C. for a State Visit where he joined President Joe Biden in marking the 70th anniversary of the U.S.-South Korean alliance. Through the Washington Declaration, the two Presidents jointly announced the establishment of a new Nuclear Consultative Group to strengthen extended deterrence, discuss nuclear and strategic planning, and manage the threat to the nonproliferation regime posed by North Korea. The latest from Belfer See more Graham Allison , Director of the Belfer Center’s Avoiding Great Power Wars Project, said in an article about Yoon’s visit and the Washington Declaration, it is “a ringing reminder of one of the greatest achievements of U.S. national security strategy: its decades-long success in preventing the proliferation of nuclear weapons. The agreement strengthened nuclear deterrence coordination between the two allies and offered a greater sense of assurance that South Korea falls under the U.S. nuclear umbrella.” Eric Rosenbach, left, adds the final signature to the formal announcement of the establishment of the Korea Foundation Endowment Fund for the Korea Project. Seated next to him, from left, Nicholas Harkness, Director of the Harvard Korea Institute, Kim Gheewhan, President of the Korea Foundation, and Mark Elliott , Vice Provost of International Affairs at Harvard University, who all also signed. In the lead-up to President Yoon’s historic Harvard visit, the Korea Project convened the 4th annual Korean Security Summit at Harvard with senior scholars and practitioners, including South Korean Foreign Minister Park Jin (MPA ‘85). At the Summit, Kim Gheewhan , President of the Korea Foundation, Eric Rosenbach , Co-Director of the Belfer Center, and Nicholas Harkness , Director of the Harvard Korea Institute, officially announced the establishment of the Korea Foundation Endowment Fund for the Korea Project. The Korea Foundation's gift, along with funding through Harvard's Korea Institute, will further strengthen and expand Korea-related policy research and security studies across Harvard. "We thank the Korea Foundation, the Korea Institute, and Christopher Jae Woo Koh , who provided a generous gift to the Korea Institute in support of expanding Korean security studies at Harvard," said Korea Project Director John Park. The Belfer Center delegation in Seoul with Unification Minister Kwon Youngse (MPA ‘01), a member of the Yoon Administration's national security team. From left: Matthew Guasco , Francesca Giovannini , Minister Kwon Youngse, Matthew Bunn , John Park , and William Tobey . ROK Ministry of Unification In May, soon after President Yoon’s visit to Harvard, the Korea Project led a delegation of Belfer Center experts to Seoul, South Korea, to present analyses on rethinking nuclear deterrence, dealing with the North Korean regime's financing of proliferation via crypto theft, and addressing the increasingly complex security situation in Northeast Asia. Led by Park, the Belfer delegation included Matthew Bunn , Principal Investigator for the Project on Managing the Atom (MTA), Francesca Giovannini , MTA Executive Director, Matthew Guasco , Postdoctoral International Security Program and MTA Fellow, and William Tobey , Belfer Senior Fellow and Director of the Office of National Security and International Studies at the Los Alamos National Laboratory. Read more about President Yoon’s Harvard address here . FEATURED IN THE SPRING 2023 NEWSLETTER Recommended citation "Belfer Center's Korea Project Co-Leads Planning for South Korean President's Historic Harvard Visit." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs and Women in Public Policy Program, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Belfer Center's Korea Project played a key role in organizing South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol's historic visit and policy speech at Harvard Kennedy School in April. The visit, which included a conversation with Professor Joseph Nye and a student Q&A, was the first by a sitting South Korean president to Harvard. This followed President Yoon's State Visit to Washington, D.C., where he and U.S. President Joe Biden marked the 70th anniversary of their alliance and announced the establishment of a Nuclear Consultative Group to strengthen extended deterrence and address North Korean threats. In the lead-up, the Korea Project convened its 4th Korean Security Summit and announced the establishment of the Korea Foundation Endowment Fund to expand Korea-related policy research at Harvard. Subsequently, the Korea Project led a delegation of Belfer Center experts to Seoul to present analyses on nuclear deterrence, North Korean crypto theft financing, and Northeast Asian security.
result.main_content_zh: 哈佛大学贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心的韩国项目，主导策划并共同赞助了韩国总统尹锡悦于四月对哈佛大学的历史性访问及其在肯尼迪学院的重要政策演讲。这是韩国现任总统首次在哈佛发表演讲，活动包括与约瑟夫·奈教授的对话及现场问答。此次哈佛之行紧接尹总统对华盛顿的国事访问，其间美韩两国元首共同纪念同盟70周年，并宣布成立新的核磋商小组，以强化延伸威慑、讨论核与战略规划，并应对朝鲜对核不扩散体系构成的威胁。访问前夕，韩国项目召开了第四届年度韩国安全峰会，并宣布设立韩国基金会捐赠基金，以加强哈佛大学内与韩国相关的政策与安全研究。此后，韩国项目还率领贝尔弗中心专家代表团访问首尔，就重新思考核威慑、应对朝鲜通过加密货币盗窃为扩散活动融资，以及处理日益复杂的东北亚安全局势等议题进行了分析汇报。
